title: Quantum Randomness: Collapse, Decoherence & Quantum Zeno

key_theorem.statement: Decoherence maps quantum superposition (EML-3: off-diagonal ρ_01 = EML-3 amplitude) to classical mixture (EML-1: diagonal ρ = Boltzmann distribution). The off-diagonal decay ρ_01(t) = ρ_01(0)·exp(-Γt) is EML-1. The pointer basis thermal state is EML-1. Thus the quantum-to-classical transition is an EML-3 → EML-1 collapse.

pure_superposition.eml: EML-3 (quantum coherence: off-diagonal = EML-3 amplitude interference)

mixed_classical.eml: EML-1 (classical mixture: diagonal only = Boltzmann probabilities)

decoherence.initial_state: EML-3 (quantum coherence: off-diagonal = EML-3 amplitude interference)

decoherence.eml_transition: EML-3 (quantum coherence) → EML-1 (classical mixture) via EML-1 exponential decay

quantum_zeno.eml_zeno_time: EML-2 (τ_Z = ħ/ΔH — inverse of standard deviation of H)

pointer_basis.eml_pointer: Pointer basis = energy eigenstates; diagonal thermal ρ = EML-1 (Boltzmann)

eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Classical thermal mixture (Boltzmann); off-diagonal decay exp(-Γt); coherent state
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Born rule P=|c|²; decoherence time τ_Z = ħ/ΔH; quantum entropy S=-Trρlogρ
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Quantum superposition ρ_01≠0; unitary evolution exp(-iHt); interference fringes
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Individual measurement outcomes (no hidden variable by Bell)

connections.to_session_70: Session 70: Born rule EML-2, Bell EML-∞. Session 81: adds decoherence + Zeno dynamics
connections.to_session_57: Decoherence → Boltzmann = EML-1; same as stat mech equilibration
connections.to_session_64: Quantum Zeno: measurement as stochastic process → Feynman-Kac style
